title: ScholarGym: Benchmarking Large Language Model Capabilities in the Information-Gathering Stage of Deep Research
abstract: ScholarGym is an evaluation environment that isolates the information-gathering stage of deep research on academic literature. It decomposes the research process into Query Planning, Tool Invocation, and Relevance Assessment, and evaluates models against 2,536 expert-annotated queries over a static corpus of 570K papers with deterministic retrieval. Experiments show that iterative query decomposition yields 2.9-3.3x F1 gains over single-query retrieval, extended thinking trades recall for precision, and Query Planning plus Relevance Assessment are dual bottlenecks.
publication_status: arXiv v3, last revised 2026-02-17; no formal venue metadata found in arXiv record
quality_notes: No public OpenReview forum was found by the repo OpenReview fetch script.
Official GitHub README lists Apache-2.0 license and datasets on HuggingFace and ModelScope.
